The claim 'Asa understands thermal stability' can be inferred indirectly: if Asa designs heat-resistant materials, then he understands thermal stability. Since Asa serves his local community and cannot both contribute to society and serve his local community, and since anyone who conducts research contributes to society, Asa must not conduct research. In fact, Asa serves his local community, and if he had expertise, he would solve real problems and contribute to society, but he cannot both contribute to society and serve his local community. Therefore, Asa cannot have expertise. Without expertise, the chain to design heat-resistant materials and thus understand thermal stability is broken.

Even if Asa lacks expertise, he might still understand thermal stability without designing heat-resistant materials. Understanding thermal stability could be separate from having expertise or designing materials. The context does not explicitly state that Asa does not understand thermal stability, so it remains uncertain rather than false.

The context defines that if someone has expertise, they solve real problems and contribute to society. Asa serves his local community and cannot both contribute to society and serve his local community, so Asa does not contribute to society. Therefore, Asa cannot have expertise. Without expertise, Asa cannot design heat-resistant materials (since that would require expertise). The conditional 'if Asa understands thermal stability, then he can design heat-resistant materials' means that if Asa understood thermal stability, he could design such materials, but without expertise, he cannot. Thus, Asa does not understand thermal stability, as it would lead to a contradiction with the no-expertise conclusion.
